text.title: Structured Interview Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Structured Interview Design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Cover 5-7 distinct competencies in the kit; fewer than 5 under-samples role demands, more than 7 dilutes per-round depth.
rule.rationale: Empirical hiring data shows correlation peaks around 5-7 competencies; outside this band signal-to-noise drops.

rule.statement: Every competency maps to exactly one primary round with a named owner; double-coverage creates redundant evidence at the cost of breadth.
rule.rationale: Mapping discipline forces explicit choices about what each round measures; redundancy is a sign of unclear scoping, not extra safety.

rule.statement: Run a dry-run with ≥2 mock candidates before the kit goes live; revise anchors where disagreement exceeds one rubric level.
rule.rationale: Live-debugging the kit on real candidates leaks bias into the cohort; dry-run is the cheap stage to fix calibration.

rule.statement: Publish the debrief contract alongside the kit (decision rule, dissent recording, deadlock break).
rule.rationale: A kit without a debrief contract decays into hallway decisions; explicit contract keeps the process auditable.

rule.statement: Audit kit annually against hire-success correlation; if correlation <0.4, rewrite anchors and re-calibrate.
rule.rationale: Kits drift as roles drift; an annual audit catches drift before it propagates into a year of bad hires.
